text:
Once upon a time, there was a small village called Willowdale. Willowdale was famous for its mysterious old manor. Every autumn, the villagers would gather near the old manorpawk to listen to his tales.

One quiet evening, little Willow listened intently as the old man spoke of an elusive bird. The bird could turn any seed green.

That night, Willow dreamed vivid dreams. In them, birds pecking green seeds fluttered through enchanted forests filled with glowing fungi. Willow awoke eagerly. The colors stayed alive longer this year.

The old man smiled. "That's your lucky star. Find it."

The next spring, Willow learned that someone was collecting rare greens. With her father's reluctant permission, Willow tracked them.

They weren't thieves. They were artists. Their dream was to decorate museums worldwide.

Willow helped collect seeds. The green became art; galleries everywhere loved it. Willow never tired of sharing her fortune. In fact...
